description: Ancient Rome was one of history's greatest civilizations, evolving from a small Italian city-state into a vast empire that dominated the Mediterranean world for over a millennium. Its legacy in law, government, engineering, and culture continues to influence the modern world.

topics.title: The Founding of Rome
topics.subtitle: Legend of Romulus and Remus and the establishment of the city

topics.title: The Roman Kingdom
topics.subtitle: Early Rome under the rule of seven legendary kings

topics.title: Birth of the Roman Republic
topics.subtitle: Overthrow of the monarchy and establishment of republican government

topics.title: The Conflict of the Orders
topics.subtitle: Struggle between patricians and plebeians for political equality

topics.title: Roman Conquest of Italy
topics.subtitle: Expansion and unification of the Italian peninsula

topics.title: The First Punic War
topics.subtitle: Rome's first major conflict with Carthage over Sicily

topics.title: Hannibal and the Second Punic War
topics.subtitle: The great Carthaginian general's invasion of Italy

topics.title: Roman Expansion in the East
topics.subtitle: Conquest of Macedonia, Greece, and Asia Minor

topics.title: The Third Punic War and Destruction of Carthage
topics.subtitle: Final elimination of Rome's greatest rival

topics.title: The Gracchi Brothers
topics.subtitle: Social reformers who challenged the Roman establishment

topics.title: Marius and Sulla
topics.subtitle: Military reforms and the first Roman civil wars

topics.title: The Rise of Julius Caesar
topics.subtitle: Conquest of Gaul and rise to power of Rome's most famous leader

topics.title: The End of the Roman Republic
topics.subtitle: Caesar's dictatorship, assassination, and the fall of republican government

topics.title: Augustus and the Birth of the Empire
topics.subtitle: Octavian becomes Augustus and establishes the Roman Empire

topics.title: The Julio-Claudian Dynasty
topics.subtitle: The first imperial dynasty including Tiberius, Caligula, Claudius, and Nero

topics.title: The Flavian Dynasty and Peak of Roman Power
topics.subtitle: Vespasian, Titus, and Domitian; construction of the Colosseum

topics.title: Trajan and Roman Imperial Expansion
topics.subtitle: The empire reaches its greatest territorial extent

topics.title: Marcus Aurelius and the End of the Golden Age
topics.subtitle: The philosopher-emperor and beginning of imperial decline

topics.title: The Crisis of the Third Century
topics.subtitle: Political chaos, economic collapse, and barbarian invasions

topics.title: Constantine and the Fall of Rome
topics.subtitle: Rise of Christianity, new capital, and the empire's final division
